name: ai-answer-card
title: AI Answer Card
description: Reviewable generated-answer surface with provenance, confidence, limitations, evidence, and review actions.

props.name: generatedLabel
props.description: Provenance badge. Pass null only where provenance is unmistakable.

props.name: metadata
props.description: Model, version, or timestamp — small print under the title.

props.name: confidence
props.description: Model-reported confidence. Rendered as metadata beside the limitations, with an explicit caveat — never as a standalone score.

props.name: limitations
props.description: What the answer does not cover and what must be checked.

props.name: evidence
props.description: Citations / provenance — typically an evidence panel.

clinicalNotes: A confidence value is metadata about a generation, not a measure of clinical correctness — 0.78 does not mean the patient's blood pressure is 78% likely to be right. The component therefore refuses to render confidence as a bare number: it always appears with that caveat and next to the limitations it qualifies. Fill `limitations` with what was NOT read ("medications, allergies, and results were not read"), since a reviewer's biggest risk is assuming an answer covered a source it never saw. Nothing here writes to a record: the action slot is caller-owned, and accepting generated content into the legal record must stay a deliberate human act.
notes: Base surface for the specialised clinical outputs — summary, note draft, medication check, differential list.
